Departs 7:00 AM · Arrives 9:59 AM

Drive to Intercontinental, check in, get settled.

Tuyet Mai

(previous option)
Ferry Building Marketplace — artisan food vendors, local produce, great for grazing. Walkable from hotel across the Embarcadero.

notes.text: SFMOMA hours: 10am–5pm daily (Thu until 9pm)

sections.label: Evening

notes.text: Hwy 120 entrance via Groveland · ~4–5 hrs from SF
Digital entrance pass required — have confirmation from recreation.gov

sections.content: Iron Door Saloon — historic gold-rush era bar and grill in Groveland. Good stopping point midway to Yosemite.

notes.text: Roughly halfway to Yosemite — good timing stop

sections.address: Curry Village (Half Dome Village), Yosemite Valley

notes.text: Valley Floor Loop: ~13 mi full loop; any section makes for a great stroll

sections.label: Dinner

deepDive.title: Yosemite Hiking Guide
deepDive.subtitle: Days 2–5 · All hikes, entry, logistics & packing list

timeline.activity: Valley Floor Loop or Vernal Fall Footbridge
Glacier Point + Sentinel Dome, or Clouds Rest

stops.label: Entry Notes — Read First
stops.address: Yosemite National Park · Hwy 120 via Groveland
stops.content:
No timed-entry reservations required for 2026 — NPS eliminated the system.

Entrance queues: Expect 60–90 min waits between 8am–5pm in peak season. Avoid by:
• Buying a digital day pass at recreation.gov (use the pre-paid lane)
• Arriving before 8am or after 5pm

Glacier Point Road: Open for the 2026 season.

Mist Trail note: The JMT "Ice Cut" between Clark Point and Panorama Trail Junction is closed through late June for repairs. The Mist Trail to Nevada Fall remains fully open — this only affects the JMT-Panorama loop variant, not your route.
tips: Digital pass at recreation.gov → skip the toll booth queue entirely
America the Beautiful pass covers entry if you have one
Tioga Road (for Clouds Rest): confirm open before planning — typically opens mid-May

notes.text: Buy digital entrance pass at recreation.gov to skip entry queues

stops.label: Valley Shuttle — Park Your Car
stops.content:
Staying at Curry Village means your car is largely irrelevant for Valley hikes. The free Valley Shuttle covers everything:

• Stop #16 — Happy Isles (Mist Trail trailhead)
• Stop #6 — Lower Yosemite Falls trailhead
• Stop #17 — Mirror Lake / Valley east
• Stop #5 — Yosemite Village / Visitor Center

Drive only for Glacier Point Road and Clouds Rest (Tioga Road).
tips: Shuttle runs frequently — expanded summer hours
Curry Village to Happy Isles (Mist Trail): ~5 min ride
Valley parking fills completely by 9am — let the shuttle handle it

stops.label: Mist Trail — Vernal & Nevada Falls
stops.content:
The single best day hike in Yosemite Valley. Ascend via the Mist Trail (steep granite staircase through Vernal Fall spray), reach Nevada Fall, descend via JMT switchbacks. Late May/early June is prime: falls at full force, wildflowers, manageable temperatures.

Route options:
• A: Vernal Fall Footbridge only — 1.6 mi RT, 1–1.5 hrs (arrival day or rest day)
• B: Top of Vernal Fall — 2.4 mi RT, ~3 hrs, strenuous
• C (recommended): Full loop Mist Trail up, JMT down — 6.4 mi, 5–6 hrs

Far fewer people past Vernal Fall. Nevada Fall viewpoint is spectacular and much less crowded.
tips: Start by 7:30am — lower trail crowds by 9am, thins significantly above Vernal Fall
Expect to get soaked on the staircase — wear rain jacket, pack phone in ziplock bag
Wet granite steps are genuinely slippery — use handrails, take your time
Carry 2L+ water; refill at Emerald Pool or Nevada Fall area (filter required)
Nevada Fall top: stay behind railings — drop is unguarded in places

notes.text: JMT "Ice Cut" closure does NOT affect the standard Mist Trail loop — your route is fully open

stops.label: Glacier Point
stops.meta: Day 4 option · Easy · 0.6 mi viewpoint · 1 hr drive each way
stops.address: Glacier Point Road (16 mi from Hwy 41 junction)
stops.content:
3,200-foot sheer drop to Yosemite Valley with Half Dome directly across. Not a hike — one of the world's great viewpoints. Best 3–5pm for warm afternoon light on Half Dome.

Add-ons from Glacier Point Road:
• Sentinel Dome (2 mi before GP): 2.2 mi RT, 400 ft, 1.5–2 hrs — 360° summit view, arguably better than Glacier Point
• Taft Point (same trailhead): 2.2 mi RT, dramatic overhanging ledge, vertiginous

Best combo: Hike Sentinel Dome → drive to Glacier Point viewpoint for sunset.
tips: Depart Curry Village by 2pm for golden afternoon light
Parking fills mid-morning on weekends — arrive before 9am or go in the afternoon

stops.label: Clouds Rest
stops.meta: Day 4 alternative · Strenuous · 12.5 mi · 2,300 ft · 6–8 hrs
stops.address: Sunrise Lakes Trailhead · Tioga Road (Hwy 120) · ~1 hr drive
stops.content:
Granite spine 1,000 ft above Half Dome — no permit lottery, no cables, no crowds. Panoramic views from 9,926 ft. The ambitious alternative to Glacier Point.

Final 0.5 miles: narrow exposed ridge with steep drop-offs on both sides. Not technical but confidence-inducing. Views from just before the ridge are still excellent if it feels too exposed.
tips: Start by 7am — thunderstorm risk builds in the afternoon on this exposed summit
Aim to summit by 11am–noon; descend immediately if clouds build
Carry 2.5–3L water from trailhead — no water at summit
Microspikes may help if snow remains in late May on north-facing sections
High elevation (~10,000 ft) — pace slower than valley elevation
Confirm Tioga Road open: nps.gov/yose/planyourvisit/conditions.htm

notes.text: Start by 7am. Descend immediately if clouds build — exposed summit in thunderstorms
Confirm Tioga Road is open before planning this hike

stops.label: Lower Yosemite Falls
stops.address: Lower Yosemite Fall Trailhead · Shuttle stop #6
stops.content:
Easy flat loop to the base of the tallest waterfall in North America. Perfect for Day 5 before checkout. Late May–early June is the best time — falls at maximum flow from snowmelt.

Take the Valley Shuttle from Curry Village to stop #6. Back at Curry Village by 9am easily.
tips: Start 7–8am on departure day — peaceful and uncrowded early
Photo: bridge at base of Lower Falls framing the full drop, and looking up the full 2,425 ft

stops.label: Valley Floor Loop
stops.content:
Flat network of paths through meadows, river crossings, and iconic viewpoints. Many of Yosemite's best photo angles are along the valley floor at dawn and dusk.

Highlights:
• Sentinel Meadow — El Capitan and Cathedral Rocks
• Valley View / El Capitan Meadow — the classic Yosemite photograph
• Cook's Meadow — Half Dome and Yosemite Falls in one frame
• Mirror Lake (shuttle #17) — Half Dome reflection on calm mornings
tips: Use the shuttle to position yourself at different points and walk sections
Dawn and dusk: best light and most wildlife (deer, black bears active)

logistics.label: Weather (Late May / Early June)
logistics.content:
Valley daytime: 65–80°F. Mornings: 45–55°F — layers needed. Afternoons: thunderstorms can build at higher elevations (especially Clouds Rest — start early).

Waterfalls at peak flow from snowmelt — Mist Trail at its most spectacular and most soaking.

Snow possible above 8,000 ft on Tioga Road routes in late May.

logistics.label: Bear Safety
logistics.content:
Black bears active in late spring:
• Store ALL food in bear boxes at Curry Village (provided at tent cabins)
• No food, trash, or scented items in your car — ever, even day bags
• On trail: bear canister for long stops

Deer common in meadows at dawn/dusk — keep distance.

logistics.label: Water on Trail
logistics.content:
Multiple sources on all Valley trails — always filter or treat (Giardia is present).

Starting load: 2L per person minimum. 2.5–3L for Clouds Rest (no water at summit).

Refill points: Merced River at Happy Isles, Emerald Pool (above Vernal Fall), Nevada Fall area.

logistics.label: Crowd Strategy
logistics.content:
Mist Trail lower section is the most crowded trail in the park. Start before 8am — crowds thin significantly above Vernal Fall at ~mile 1.5.

Your window (Fri May 30 – Mon June 2) is mixed: weekend days are busier. Early starts solve most of it.

checklists.label: 🎒 Packing List
items: Lightweight packable rain jacket — essential for Mist Trail
Light fleece or mid-layer for mornings and high elevation
Hiking shoes with grip (wet granite on Mist Trail is slippery)
Trekking poles — helpful on Mist Trail descent
Downloaded offline Yosemite map (AllTrails or Gaia GPS)
Microspikes — if attempting Clouds Rest in late May
Snacks and trail lunch (no food services on trail)
Ziplock bag for phone — Mist Trail spray
Bear box usage at Curry Village — mandatory

sections.label: Breakfast
sections.content: Curry Village Market — grab supplies and pack a trail lunch for the hike.

notes.text: Pack lunch, snacks, and plenty of water — long hike day ahead

sections.label: Morning
sections.content: Mist Trail — Vernal Fall + Nevada Fall extension. One of Yosemite's iconic hikes. Expect to get soaked near the falls — bring a rain jacket or poncho.

notes.text: 6–9 miles total, moderate-to-strenuous. Bring rain jacket/poncho for the mist.
Vernal Fall: ~3 mi RT · Nevada Fall extension: ~7 mi RT
Start early to beat crowds and afternoon heat

sections.label: Lunch
sections.content: Trail lunch at Nevada Fall viewpoint or Vernal Fall footbridge.

sections.label: Afternoon
sections.content: Return from hike, rest at Curry Village. Optional gentle Valley exploration.

sections.label: Dinner
sections.content: Curry Village Pizza Deck or Bar 1899.

sections.label: Evening
sections.content: Night walk in the Valley or rest. Watch for deer.

deepDive.title: Mist Trail Deep Dive
deepDive.subtitle: Vernal & Nevada Falls Loop · 6.4 mi · 2,000 ft · 5–6 hrs

timeline.activity: Back at Happy Isles via JMT

stops.label: Getting There
stops.meta: Take the shuttle — do not drive
stops.content:
Valley parking fills completely by 9am. Take the free Valley Shuttle from Curry Village to Stop #16 (Happy Isles trailhead).

~5 min ride. The shuttle drops you right at the start of the trail.

stops.label: The Mist Trail Staircase
stops.meta: Mile 0 to ~1.5 · 1,000 ft gain · where you get soaked
stops.content:
The famous granite staircase climbs directly through Vernal Fall mist. In late May, this is heavy spray — not drizzle. You will get soaked from the knees up.

The steps are wet, polished granite. They are genuinely slippery — use the handrails and take your time. Slipping accidents on this section happen every season.

Above the staircase: Emerald Pool — beautiful calm water above the fall. Excellent rest stop and refill point.
tips: Put rain jacket on before the staircase starts — faster than stopping mid-climb
Pack phone in a ziplock bag before you leave Curry Village
Trekking poles useful here for extra stability on descent and wet steps
Crowds thin significantly past the top of Vernal Fall (~mile 1.5) — push through

notes.text: Wet granite steps — use handrails, no rushing, watch footing

stops.label: Nevada Fall
stops.meta: Mile 3.2 · 2,000 ft total gain · the payoff
stops.content:
Nevada Fall viewpoint is one of the finest views in the park — Half Dome directly across, the Valley spread far below. This is where the crowds disappear entirely.

Take your time here. Eat lunch. This is the point.
tips: Stay behind railings at the overlook — drop is unguarded in places
Photo: Half Dome framed directly behind Nevada Fall from the overlook
Photo: Liberty Cap and Nevada Fall together on the JMT descent

notes.text: Nevada Fall top: stay behind railings — drop is unguarded in places

stops.label: JMT Descent
stops.meta: Nevada Fall → Happy Isles · easier on knees than out-and-back
stops.content:
Descend via the John Muir Trail switchbacks — more gradual than the Mist Trail, much kinder on your knees. This is why the loop is better than out-and-back.

The JMT passes Clark Point, another excellent viewpoint en route down.

NOTE: The JMT "Ice Cut" closure (Clark Point to Panorama Junction) does NOT affect this descent — your route to Happy Isles is fully open.
tips: Trekking poles help significantly on the descent if knees are feeling the climb
Keep a steady pace — it's easy to go too fast on switchbacks and blow out your knees

logistics.label: What to Pack Today
logistics.content:
• Rain jacket — on before the Mist Trail staircase
• Ziplock bag for phone
• 2L+ water per person; refill at Emerald Pool or Nevada Fall (filter required)
• Trail lunch
• Trekking poles — optional but useful on the staircase and descent
• Hiking shoes with grip — wet granite does not forgive trail runners

logistics.label: Photo Spots
logistics.content:
1. Vernal Fall footbridge — classic framed view of the fall
2. Top of Mist Trail staircase — looking back down the valley
3. Emerald Pool above Vernal Fall — turquoise water, dramatic walls
4. Nevada Fall overlook — Half Dome centered in background
5. JMT descent — Liberty Cap and Nevada Fall together

logistics.label: Water Sources
logistics.content:
• Merced River at Happy Isles trailhead
• Vernal Fall footbridge area
• Emerald Pool (above Vernal Fall) — filter required
• Nevada Fall area — filter required

Carry at least 2L from the start. Refill and filter at Emerald Pool if running low before Nevada Fall.

sections.label: Breakfast
sections.content: Curry Village Meadow Grill — outdoor grill, good for a hearty morning meal.

sections.label: Morning
sections.content:
Option A — Glacier Point overlook: drive up (~1 hr), stunning 360° views of Half Dome, the Valley, and the High Sierra. Easy walk at the top.

Option B — Clouds Rest hike: strenuous ridge hike above Half Dome elevation with incredible panoramic views.

notes.text: Glacier Point: ~1 hr drive from Curry Village · Overlook at 7,214 ft
Clouds Rest: ~14 mi RT, strenuous — best if legs recovered from Mist Trail

sections.label: Lunch
sections.content: Degnan's Kitchen — deli-style sandwiches and quick bites in Yosemite Village.
sections.address: Yosemite Village (near Visitor Center), Yosemite Valley

sections.label: Afternoon
sections.content: Explore the Valley at leisure — Yosemite Village, Mirror Lake trail, or rest.

sections.label: Dinner
sections.content: Curry Village Pizza Deck or Bar 1899.

sections.label: Evening
sections.content: Ahwahnee Bar — sundowners at the historic Ahwahnee Hotel. Open to the public; no hotel stay required. Worth dressing up slightly.

notes.text: Ahwahnee Bar — verify cash-only before going
Open to public — no hotel stay required

timeline.note: Half day · easy · stunning sunset views

stops.label: Glacier Point
stops.meta: Easy · 0.6 mi viewpoint · depart by 2pm for sunset light
stops.address: End of Glacier Point Road · ~1 hr drive from Curry Village
stops.content:
3,200-foot sheer drop to Yosemite Valley with Half Dome directly across. Not a hike — one of the world's great viewpoints. Open for the 2026 season.

Best visited 3–5pm for warm afternoon light on Half Dome. Sunset here is exceptional — plan to linger.
tips: Depart Curry Village by 2pm to catch golden afternoon light on Half Dome
Parking fills by mid-morning on weekends — arriving in the afternoon is actually ideal
Faces west into the Valley — sunset colors are extraordinary

stops.label: Sentinel Dome (add-on)
stops.address: Sentinel Dome Trailhead — 2 miles before Glacier Point on the road
stops.content:
360-degree panoramic summit — El Capitan, Half Dome, the entire high Sierra. Arguably a better view than Glacier Point itself, with far fewer people.

Easy to combine: hike Sentinel Dome first, then drive the remaining 2 miles to Glacier Point viewpoint. Best Day 4 pairing.
tips: Stop at the Sentinel Dome trailhead before reaching Glacier Point parking
Taft Point shares this same trailhead: dramatic overhanging ledge, vertiginous — not for heights-averse
Doing both Sentinel Dome + Taft Point is a solid 3–4 hr outing before the Glacier Point drive

stops.label: Clouds Rest
stops.address: Sunrise Lakes Trailhead · Tioga Road (Hwy 120) · ~1 hr drive
stops.content:
Granite spine 1,000 ft above Half Dome — no permit lottery, no cables, no crowds. Panoramic views from 9,926 ft. The most ambitious option — commit to a full day.

First challenge: steep switchbacks ~1.5 miles in, gaining ~1,000 ft. Sustained but not technical.

Final 0.5 miles: narrow exposed granite ridge, steep drop-offs on both sides. Not technical — but bold. Views from just before the ridge are still excellent if it feels too exposed.

High elevation (~10,000 ft) means thinner air — budget extra time.
tips: Start by 7am — thunderstorms build fast on this exposed summit in the afternoon
Aim to be at summit by 11am–noon; descend immediately if clouds build
Carry 2.5–3L water from trailhead — no water sources at summit
Microspikes may help on north-facing snow patches in late May
~1 hr drive from Curry Village via Big Oak Flat Road → Tioga Road
Confirm Tioga Road is open: nps.gov/yose/planyourvisit/conditions.htm

notes.text: Start by 7am. Descend immediately if clouds build — no shelter on the exposed summit ridge
Confirm Tioga Road is open before planning this hike

logistics.label: Glacier Point vs. Clouds Rest
logistics.content:
Glacier Point: 30 min from the car, iconic Half Dome view in comfort. Perfect if yesterday's Mist Trail left you tired or if you want a relaxed afternoon.

Clouds Rest: 6–8 hour full-day commitment, physically higher than Half Dome, more dramatic and earned. Best if you recovered well and want one more big day in the mountains.

Both are worth doing — on different days if schedule allows.

logistics.label: Getting There
logistics.content:
Glacier Point: Drive from Curry Village — Hwy 41 south, then Glacier Point Road (16 miles). ~1 hr. No shuttle service to Glacier Point.

Clouds Rest: Drive from Curry Village — Big Oak Flat Road north, then Tioga Road (Hwy 120) east to Sunrise Lakes Trailhead. ~1 hr. Confirm Tioga Road open before going.

sections.label: Breakfast
sections.content: Curry Village Market — last Yosemite morning.

sections.label: Morning
sections.content: Lower Yosemite Falls — short, easy hike (1 mi loop). Great farewell to the Valley.
sections.address: Lower Yosemite Fall Trailhead, Yosemite Valley

notes.text: ~1 mile loop, easy — perfect relaxed morning before the drive
Best water flow late May/early June (peak snowmelt)

sections.label: Lunch
sections.content: Curry Village Meadow Grill — final Yosemite meal before departure.

sections.label: Drive
sections.content: Depart Yosemite for Mill Valley — ~3.5–4 hrs via Hwy 120 west to the Bay Area.

notes.text: ~3.5–4 hrs · Exit via Hwy 120 west (Groveland) · Golden Gate Bridge or Richmond Bridge into Marin

sections.label: Dinner
sections.content: Dinner with friends in Mill Valley.

sections.label: Evening
sections.content: Settle in at friends' place in Mill Valley.

sections.label: Breakfast
sections.content: Tartine Manufactory — legendary SF bakery. Exceptional coffee and pastries. Arrive early to beat the line.

notes.text: Arrive by 8:15am to beat the line

sections.label: Morning
sections.content:
Valencia Street — wander north from ~16th St:

• Paletería Nora (16th & Valencia) — Mexican paletas
• Bi-Rite Market (18th & Valencia) — beloved neighborhood grocery & deli
• Noun Vintage (20th & Valencia) — curated vintage clothing
• Costume & Cake (22nd & Valencia) — specialty shop
sections.address: Valencia St from 16th to 22nd St, San Francisco

notes.text: ~1.5 hrs, roughly 0.5 mi stroll · Most shops open by 11am

sections.label: Lunch
sections.content: Good Mong Kok Bakery — classic Chinatown dim sum takeout. Order at the counter.

notes.text: Cash preferred — bring bills
Very busy 11:30am–1:30pm — arrive before noon if possible
18 stools, mostly takeout · Order at counter

sections.label: Afternoon
sections.content: Red Blossom Tea Company — exceptional tea shop on Grant Ave. Browse and buy; in-store tastings are no longer offered.

notes.text: In-store tastings discontinued — browse and buy only
~1.25 hrs to browse their full selection

sections.label: Late Afternoon
sections.content:
City Lights Bookstore — iconic Beat Generation bookshop in North Beach. Excellent poetry and literature. Washington Square Park is 2 blocks away.

A few blocks south in Jackson Square:
• Tailors' Keep (618 Washington St) — sharp men's tailoring and ready-to-wear
• Quince (470 Pacific Ave) — Michelin-starred Californian fine dining; worth booking if you want to stay in the city for dinner tonight
sections.address: City Lights: 261 Columbus Ave · Tailors' Keep: 618 Washington St · Quince: 470 Pacific Ave

notes.text: ~1.5 hrs · Walking total today: ~2 miles (Valencia + Chinatown + North Beach)
Quince: reservation required if staying in SF for dinner

sections.label: Evening
sections.content:
Return to Mill Valley for dinner with friends.

Transit options:
• BART from Civic Center (~15 min walk) or Powell St
• Rideshare from North Beach: ~30–40 min, ~$40–60

notes.text: BART: Civic Center or Powell St stations · Transfer at Embarcadero for ferry to Marin

deepDive.title: SF Tea, Art & Neighborhood Deep Dive
deepDive.subtitle: Valencia Street + Chinatown Tea Route

stops.label: Tartine Manufactory
stops.content: Iconic pastry destination. Sourdough croissants, excellent coffee. Worth the early wake.
tips: Arrive by 8:15am to beat the line
Grab a sourdough croissant and a pastry to share
Coffee is solid; tea not a priority here
~20 min to eat, then walk or transit to Valencia

stops.label: Valencia Street Wander
stops.content: Walk north from ~16th St. No fixed route — wander, dip into shops that appeal. Budget ~1.5hrs; you'll cover maybe 10–12 blocks.
tips: Paletería Nora (16th & Valencia) — colorful Mexican paletas
Bi-Rite Market (18th & Valencia) — high-end grocer, beautiful design
Noun Vintage (20th & Valencia) — curated vintage clothes
Costume & Cake (22nd & Valencia) — quirky bookstore/café
Dandelion Chocolate (18th & Guerrero, just west) — if craving chocolate
Stop at intersections for murals and photos

stops.label: Walk to Chinatown
stops.content: Two route options depending on energy and time.
tips: Option A (scenic): Valencia → Mission Dolores Park → Grant Ave → Chinatown — ~2 mi, ~45–50 min walking
Option B (faster): Valencia → Market St east → Stockton St north — ~1.5 mi, ~30–35 min walking
Take Option A if energy is high; Option B if you want more time in Chinatown

stops.label: Good Mong Kok Bakery
stops.content: Legendary dim sum bakery. Point at what you want at the counter. Budget $8–15 per person for 3–4 items.
tips: Signature items: BBQ pork bun, egg tart, shrimp dumpling
Cash preferred — ATM on Stockton nearby
Lines move fast despite the crowds
Take to go; eat at nearby benches if available

notes.text: Cash preferred
Peak hours 11:30am–1:30pm — you'll hit the tail end; move efficiently

stops.label: Red Blossom Tea Company
stops.content: Treat this as a focused buying trip, not a tasting. Staff are knowledgeable — ask directly. Lead with your collection gaps vs. what you already source from Yunnan Sourcing and white2tea.
tips: Strategy: identify gaps vs. your YS/w2t collection before entering
Ask staff: "What would you prioritize for an oolong and pu-erh collector?"
Request to smell dry leaf before committing — they allow this
Taiwanese oolongs (their strength): Alishan, Dong Ding, Li Shan, Oriental Beauty — $15–60/50g
HK-style aged pu-erh: humid storage profile — good contrast to YS dry storage — $20–80+
Chinese greens (reliable): Longjing, Biluochun — $10–35
White tea: Fuding Baimudan and Shoumei — smaller selection but curated — $12–40
Budget $50–120 for 2–3 focused purchases; avoid over-buying (travel storage is limited)
Grab their card for future online orders

notes.text: In-store tastings discontinued — browse and buy only
Ask about harvest date and storage conditions for any aged items

stops.label: City Lights Bookstore
stops.content: Legendary Beat Generation bookshop. Three dense floors. Poetry section (rear, upper floor) is the heart of the place.
tips: Wander all three floors — not huge but very dense
Poetry section is rear, upper floor — City Lights' legacy
Washington Square Park: 2 blocks away at Filbert & Stockton
Saints Peter and Paul Church: gorgeous white church nearby, great photo
Cafe Trieste (nearby): historic Italian café, atmospheric if touristy

logistics.label: Transit Back to Mill Valley
logistics.content:
• BART: Walk to Civic Center or Powell St → BART to Embarcadero → Ferry or rideshare to Marin
• Rideshare direct: ~30–40 min, ~$40–60
• Golden Gate Ferry: BART → Ferry Building → ferry to Marin (check schedule)

Recommendation: Check ferry schedule before leaving. If you'll miss it, rideshare is faster and less stressful — you have a dinner to make.

logistics.label: Pacing & Energy
logistics.content:
This is a FULL day on foot. Key notes:

• 8–9am Tartine: High energy, standing
• 9:30–11am Valencia: Moderate pace, meander and sit
• 11am–12:30pm Walk to Chinatown: 45–50 min on feet — grab coffee en route if low
• 12:30–1:15pm Good Mong Kok: Quick refuel
• 1:15–2:30pm Red Blossom: Slow-paced browsing — perfect timing for the 2pm energy dip
• 2:30–4pm City Lights: Standing, unhurried
• 4–5:30pm Transit: Rest time

logistics.label: What to Bring
logistics.content:
• Comfortable walking shoes (2+ miles of strolling)
• Small backpack or crossbody bag (for light purchases)
• Cash (Good Mong Kok, Red Blossom)
• Water bottle (refill at cafés)
• Camera (Valencia murals, North Beach aesthetics)
• Layers — SF June mornings are chilly, afternoons warm up

logistics.label: What to Skip / Alternatives
logistics.content:
If energy is low: Skip Valencia; replace with rest or a second coffee stop. Extend Red Blossom visit.

If compressed for time: Cut North Beach/City Lights; extend Red Blossom and grab tea at a Chinatown café.

Rain day: Use BART more liberally; skip outdoor walking sections; Tartine + Red Blossom + City Lights all work in rain.

checklists.label: 🍵 Red Blossom Tea — Browsing Checklist
items: Identify gaps vs. your YS/w2t collection before entering
Ask staff: "What's best for an oolong and pu-erh collector?"
Request dry-leaf smell before committing to a purchase
Taiwanese oolongs: ask about Alishan, Dong Ding, Li Shan, Oriental Beauty
Aged pu-erh: ask about humid-storage options (contrast to YS dry storage)
Ask about harvest date and storage conditions for any aged items
Request heat-sealed bags for loose-leaf purchases
Grab their card for future online orders

sections.label: Breakfast
sections.content: Song Tea & Ceramics — intimate tea tasting experience. Reservation via Tock required.

notes.text: Tock reservation required — confirm your booking before leaving

sections.label: Morning
sections.content:
Fillmore Street corridor — Japantown through Pacific Heights and into Russian Hill.

• Studio Ferris (1504 Vallejo St) — menswear right at Fillmore & Vallejo, Japanese-influenced tailoring
• Caravane SF (3461 Sacramento St) — beautiful home goods and interiors, a short detour west on Sacramento
• Presidio Post (1301 Mason St) — Spyplane-endorsed clothing, a few blocks east toward Polk/Russian Hill
sections.address: Fillmore St from Sutter to Broadway · Studio Ferris: 1504 Vallejo · Caravane: 3461 Sacramento · Presidio Post: 1301 Mason

sections.label: Lunch
sections.content: Swan Oyster Depot — legendary SF raw bar. Counter-only. Cash only. Closes 2:30pm sharp. Order fresh oysters, Dungeness crab, clam chowder.

notes.text: Cash only — ATM nearby on Polk St
Closes 2:30pm sharp — arrive by noon; line forms early
18 counter stools only · No reservations

sections.label: Afternoon
sections.content:
Option A — Fraenkel Gallery (49 Geary St): check website for current June exhibition before visiting.

Option B — Evan Kinori (1367 Valencia St): Japanese-influenced menswear, open Thu 12–6pm only.

Option C — Golden Gate Park: de Young Museum + Japanese Tea Garden + walk to Ocean Beach. HI NRG (443 Clement St, Inner Richmond) is a great coffee stop before heading in. Rising Star Laundry (858 Cole St, Cole Valley) is worth a detour if approaching from the Haight side.

Option D — Hayes Valley pre-Zuni: Modern Appealing Clothing (387 Grove St) + Reliquary (544 Hayes St) + LOQUAT (198 Gough St) for coffee/pastry. All within a 5-min walk of each other and Zuni. Tacos Oscar (530 Haight St) if you want a light bite before the chicken.
sections.address: Fraenkel: 49 Geary · Evan Kinori: 1367 Valencia · HI NRG: 443 Clement · MAC: 387 Grove · Reliquary: 544 Hayes

notes.text: Fraenkel Gallery: Check website for current June exhibition and hours
Evan Kinori: Open Thu 12–6pm only · Closed Wednesdays
de Young + Japanese Tea Garden: plan ~2–3 hrs for the full park visit
Hayes Valley cluster (Option D) is a 5-min walk from Zuni — easy to roll straight into dinner

sections.label: Dinner
sections.content: Zuni Café — roast chicken for two is the signature dish. Order immediately on arrival — it takes ~1 hour. Warm, wood-fired room, excellent wine list.

notes.text: Order roast chicken for two on arrival — ~1 hr wait for the dish
Classic SF bistro atmosphere · Great natural wine list

sections.label: Evening
sections.content: Return to Mill Valley after dinner.

notes.text: Rideshare from Market St to Mill Valley: ~30–40 min

deepDive.title: Song Tea Morning
deepDive.subtitle: Gong fu tasting + teaware browsing · Lower Pacific Heights

timeline.note: Song ceramics line + Red Blossom if needed

stops.label: Song Tea & Ceramics
stops.content: Intimate gong fu tasting bar — Peter Luong or staff guide you through 2–4 teas with multiple infusions each. Exceptional Taiwanese and Fujian sourcing: high-mountain oolongs (Lishan, Shan Lin Xi), dan cong, rock oolongs (Wuyi). Ask about current white and green tea offerings. Ceramics displayed throughout are also for sale.
tips: Book on Tock as early as possible — slots fill weeks out
Add a note on Tock flagging your collection background for a more tailored session
Confirm reservation 24–48 hours before; some slots require day-of reconfirmation
Relaxed, unhurried pace — multiple infusions per tea, not a quick flight format
Bring a small notebook for tasting notes: tea name, origin, harvest, aroma, finish, would-buy
Budget for ceramics separately if you're likely to buy — Song's own line is gallery-quality
Wed–Sun 9am–5pm; confirm hours before visiting

notes.text: Reservation required — book on Tock: exploretock.com/songtea
Est. cost $25–45/person; confirm current pricing on Tock

stops.label: Teaware Scouting
stops.meta: What to look for in SF
stops.content: Song and Red Blossom are the two legitimate teaware sources. Most Chinatown shops sell decorative, not functional ware — avoid heavy patterns or suspiciously low prices. Look for unglazed Yixing-style or plain white porcelain.
tips: Gaiwans: thin porcelain lip (thicker burns fingers), lid sits flush, ~100ml for gong fu
Check interior glaze for pinholes or uneven coverage
Cups: 30–60ml, smooth interior glaze, thin walls preferred
Storage vessels: sealed tin or glazed ceramic — avoid plastic for long-term storage
Song's ceramic line: functional and well-made, higher price but excellent craftsmanship
Red Blossom accessory section: practical travel-grade gaiwans and basic cup sets

notes.text: Chinatown tourist shops: decorative only — skip unless you find plain porcelain at quality price

logistics.label: Travel Storage for Purchases
logistics.content:
Loose-leaf: request heat-sealed bags; store separately from luggage with strong odors.
Pu-erh: leave in original wrapping until home storage.
Loose oolongs: airtight zip-locks.
Teaware: wrap in soft clothing in carry-on — never checked bag.

logistics.label: Pacing Note
logistics.content: Song Tea runs 60–90 min. Don't schedule anything immediately after — the session is meant to be unhurried. Japantown is a short walk or rideshare east; GG Park is a 10–15 min ride west.

checklists.label: 🫖 Teaware Priority List
items: 100ml gaiwan (backup or upgrade if Song has a notable piece)
Pouring pitcher (gongdao bei) — if useful for home setup
Skip Yixing teapots — hard to verify authenticity in SF

sections.label: Drive
sections.content: Mill Valley → Healdsburg — ~1h 15m via Hwy 101 north through Marin and Sonoma.

notes.text: ~1h 15m · Hwy 101 north · The Lodge at Healdsburg on arrival

sections.label: Morning
sections.content: Healdsburg Plaza — beautiful Spanish colonial town square. Coffee, browse shops, get oriented.

sections.label: Lunch
sections.content: Dry Creek General Store — classic California roadhouse, great sandwiches and local atmosphere.

notes.text: Classic stop en route to the Dry Creek Valley wineries

sections.label: Afternoon
sections.content:
Dry Creek Valley wineries:

• A. Rafanelli Winery — family-owned, exceptional Zinfandel, appointment preferred
• Preston Farm & Winery — organic, biodynamic, old-vine Zinfandel and Rhône varieties
• Healdsburg Farmers' Market (if timing works)
sections.address: A. Rafanelli: 4685 W Dry Creek Rd · Preston: 9282 W Dry Creek Rd

notes.text: A. Rafanelli: Appointment preferred — call ahead
Dry Creek Valley is compact — wineries are close together on West Dry Creek Rd

sections.label: Welcome Party
sections.content:
Preston Farm & Winery — 6:00–9:00 PM.

Dress: Casual Chic — sundresses, skirts and tops, jumpsuits; trousers and t-shirts, denim and jackets.
sections.address: Preston Farm & Winery, West Dry Creek Rd, Healdsburg, CA

notes.text: Wear sturdy shoes and bring layers

sections.label: Morning
sections.content: Free morning — Healdsburg Plaza, coffee, rest up before the big event.

sections.label: The Wedding
sections.content:
Ceremony at 4:00 PM.

Dress: Formal with Flair — suits and dresses with fun colors, patterns, or accessories.
sections.address: 4250 West Dry Creek Road, Healdsburg, CA

notes.text: Wear sturdy shoes and bring layers

sections.label: Farewell Brunch
sections.content:
Farewell Brunch — 10:00 AM to 1:00 PM.

Dress: Sunday Morning Picnic Attire.
sections.address: Dragonfly Farm & Floral, Westside Rd, Healdsburg, CA

notes.text: ~1h 15m drive from Healdsburg to SFO — depart by 11:00 AM to make your flight

sections.label: Departure
sections.content:
UA 2480 · SFO → EWR
Departs 1:50 PM · Arrives 10:26 PM
Boeing 787-9 Dreamliner · Seat 34C
